name: Coit Tower

permits.description: bldg rehabilitation, exterior repair, door & window rehabilitation, interior finishes paint & tile repair. upgrade for mech & elect, accessibility, hand & guardrail, restroom. new accessible & site signage. ($50k non barrier removal items, others are maintenance & accessibility upgrade work)
(rear building) to erect 1 story, no basement, limited restaurant.
paint interior of existing shop, remove/replace (e) slatwall, add 3 new outlets to (e) circuit, add new cabinets, add 2 new audio/visual alarms exit signage, ada accessible counter top & ada signage.

summary_en: Coit Tower's civic record centers on its fabric and its art. A 1989 Historic Structure Report, prepared for the Arts Commission, inventories the tower's exterior walls, the Columbus-statue planter, the tower shaft and the belvedere level for preservation. A 1989 guide to the city's Civic Art Collection places the tower's murals under the Arts Commission's aesthetic-review authority — no structure on public land, it notes, receives a building permit without that review. Recreation & Park Commission minutes from 1986, which index several Coit Tower resolutions, record the passage of the Prop. 43 Park Bond, an application to the state Office of Historic Preservation, and arrangements for groups to view the murals. The tower is operated by the Recreation & Parks Department, whose FY2025 department budget totals about $250M citywide.
